summary: Plan is structurally sound and line references are accurate. Two required changes: Step 3 must fix the toroidal wrap bug (not just audit it), and Step 2 needs a clearer muted-detection mechanism.

stepsReview.title: Add frame-to-frame bass smoothing in renderLoop
stepsReview.notes: Line references confirmed: bass is a bare local reset each frame (line 85), no EMA exists. Lerp factor 0.15 is reasonable. Smoothing raw 0-255 values before passing to mapSignalsToVisuals is correct since bassToMacro normalizes downstream. Applying same treatment to treble is a good consistency call.

stepsReview.title: Add synthetic bass fallback when muted
stepsReview.notes: Concept is correct — gain=0 produces exact-zero bins so the detection heuristic works. However, the plan should clarify: check the pipeline's frequency array sum, not just 'all bins are 0', since a sum check is cheaper and more robust. Also, the synthetic values (40 + 30 * sin) are in the 0-255 raw range and will flow through bassToMacro normalization — this is correct but should be explicitly noted to prevent future confusion.
requiredChanges: Use a sum-of-bins threshold (e.g., sum < 1) rather than checking every bin individually for zero
Add an inline comment noting synthetic values are in raw 0-255 scale, pre-normalization

stepsReview.title: Verify and harden particleField bass-driven motion
stepsReview.notes: The plan states 'wrapping + clamping already in place' and 'no code changes expected'. This is incorrect. The toroidal wrap at lines 66-73 uses single-step if-statements (if x < 0, x += 1), not modulo. When bassBoost=3.0 and delta is large (e.g., tab-resume spike), a particle can move more than 1.0 unit in a single frame. The single if-add won't fully re-wrap, and the hard clamp on lines 72-73 will pin particles to edges instead of wrapping. This violates AC4 (no clipping or visual instability).
requiredChanges: Replace single-step if-wrap with modulo-based wrapping: p.x = ((p.x % 1) + 1) % 1 (handles any magnitude)
Or add a while-loop wrap for the if-based approach
Add a delta clamp (e.g., cap delta at 100ms) in renderLoop to prevent tab-resume spikes from causing extreme displacement — this also benefits Step 1 smoothing stability

stepsReview.title: Write tests for US-018 acceptance criteria
stepsReview.notes: Test IDs and coverage are well-designed. T-018-04 should specifically test the large-delta + max-bass scenario that exposes the wrapping bug. T-018-03 needs to test the synthetic fallback path, not just that bassEnergy > 0.
requiredChanges: T-018-04: Include a sub-case with delta=500ms + bassEnergy=1.0 to stress the toroidal wrap fix
T-018-03: Verify the synthetic bass oscillates (test at two different timestamps and confirm different values)

missingConsiderations.recommendation: Add a delta clamp (cap at ~100ms) at the top of the frame function in renderLoop.ts. This prevents tab-resume spikes from causing extreme particle displacement, smoothing instability, and synthetic bass phase jumps. Should be added to Step 1 or Step 3.

verifiedAssumptions: computeBassAvg at lines 53-59 — confirmed, bottom 25% of FFT bins
bassToMacro at lines 165-170 — confirmed, power curve 0.7 with 255 normalization
VisualParams.bassEnergy at line 32 of mappings.ts — confirmed
No existing frame-to-frame smoothing anywhere in the pipeline — confirmed
No muted-state fallback — confirmed, hard-zero only
Existing tests T-008-14, T-008-16, T-009-24 — confirmed present

invalidAssumptions.claim: Toroidal wrapping handles any bassBoost magnitude
invalidAssumptions.reality: Single-step if-wrap fails when displacement exceeds 1.0 per frame; hard clamp masks the failure by pinning to edges
